seoTitle: Fusemi (Senwei) Products | IGBT Modules, MOSFETs, SiC Devices Distributor
seoDescription: Browse Fusemi power semiconductor products including IGBT modules, MOSFETs, and SiC devices. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What is the difference between IGBT and MOSFET?
faqs.answer: IGBT and MOSFET are both power semiconductor switches: MOSFETs are better for <600V applications with fast switching and low switching losses. IGBTs are better for >600V applications with lower conduction losses at high power. For 600V-1700V applications, IGBTs are typically preferred. For <600V, MOSFETs offer better efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MOSFETs for <600V high-efficiency applications. Use IGBTs for >600V high-power applications.

faqs.question: How do I select the right IGBT module?
faqs.answer: IGBT module selection: 1) Voltage rating - 2-3x maximum DC link voltage; 2) Current rating - 1.5-2x margin for overload; 3) Switching frequency - lower Eoff for high frequency; 4) Package type - standard, dual, or six-pack; 5) Temperature rating - ensure adequate derating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select voltage 2-3x system voltage with current margin. Contact our FAE for complex applications.

faqs.question: What are SiC advantages over silicon?
faqs.answer: SiC advantages: 1) Higher efficiency - lower switching and conduction losses; 2) Higher operating temperatures - junction temps up to 200°C+; 3) Higher breakdown voltage - 10x higher than silicon; 4) Better thermal conductivity; 5) Smaller size - higher power density. SiC is ideal for EV, solar, and high-efficiency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider SiC for high-efficiency applications where performance justifies higher cost.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Si MOSFET and SiC MOSFET?
faqs.answer: Si MOSFETs operate up to 600V with excellent efficiency at low voltages. SiC MOSFETs operate up to 1700V+ with much lower switching losses and higher temperature capability. SiC has faster switching and lower losses, especially at high frequencies and voltages. SiC costs more but enables smaller, more efficient systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Si MOSFETs for <600V. Use SiC for >600V or high-frequency high-efficiency applications.

faqs.question: What package types are available for IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: Common IGBT module packages: 1) Standard modules (62mm) - versatile, widely used; 2) Mini modules (34mm) - compact for lower power; 3) PrimePACK - high power with integrated diode; 4) Easy modules - economic compact design; 5) Six-pack modules - integrated inverter for motor control. Package choice depends on power level, cooling, and application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose package based on power level, cooling method, and application. Contact our FAE for guidance.

categories.name: IGBT Modules
categories.description: Fusemi IGBT modules provide reliable power switching for industrial drives, EV inverters, and solar inverters. Available in voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V with various package options including standard modules, dual modules, and six-pack configurations.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select IGBT Modules
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting IGBT modules based on voltage, current, switching frequency, and application requirements.

faqs.question: What voltage rating do I need?
faqs.answer: Select IGBT voltage rating 2-3x maximum DC link voltage. For 400V AC systems (565V DC), use 1200V IGBT. For 690V AC systems (1000V DC), use 1700V IGBT. Higher voltage ratings provide safety margin for voltage transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2-3x voltage margin. 1200V for 400V systems, 1700V for 690V systems.

products.name: 50A 1200V IGBT Module
products.shortDescription: 50A 1200V IGBT module in standard package for motor drives and power supplies.
descriptionParagraphs: The SWA50-1200T is a high-performance IGBT module designed for industrial motor drive applications.
With 1200V rating and 50A continuous current, this module provides reliable power switching for 400V class systems.
The standard 62mm package enables easy integration into existing designs.
features: Low VCE(sat) for reduced conduction losses

faeReview.author: Dr. James Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: In my 18 years supporting power electronics designs, I've found Fusemi IGBT modules to offer excellent value for industrial applications. The SWA50-1200T is ideal for 5-15kW motor drives where the standard 62mm package simplifies thermal management. The integrated diodes are robust and handle the reverse recovery well. For sizing, I recommend 1.5-2x current margin for motor starting conditions. The module's switching characteristics are well-balanced for 5-10kHz operation common in industrial drives.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 1200V IGBT module with excellent value for industrial motor drive applications

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current for larger power ratings

faqs.question: What gate resistor value should I use?
faqs.answer: Gate resistor selection balances switching speed and EMI. Typical values: 5-15 ohms for standard IGBTs. Lower resistance = faster switching + more EMI. Higher resistance = slower switching + less EMI. Start with 10 ohms and adjust based on observed waveforms and temperatures. Consider using gate resistors with soft recovery diodes for reduced ringing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 10 ohms gate resistor. Adjust based on switching performance.

products.name: 150A 1200V IGBT Module High Power
products.shortDescription: 150A 1200V high-power IGBT module for EV inverters and solar inverters.
descriptionParagraphs: The SWH150-1200H is a high-power IGBT module designed for demanding applications including EV inverters and solar inverters.
With 1200V rating and 150A continuous current, this module handles high power levels with robust thermal performance.
The enhanced package design provides improved thermal resistance for continuous high-power operation.
features: 150A high current rating for demanding applications
Low switching losses for high efficiency

faeReview.author: Dr. Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - EV Power Systems
faeReview.content: The SWH150-1200H is an excellent choice for EV inverter applications. I've used this module in several 30-50kW inverter designs where the 150A rating provides good margin for peak power conditions. The short circuit withstand of 10μs is critical for EV applications where short circuits must be managed safely. The thermal performance is adequate for forced-air cooling in most vehicle applications. For water-cooled designs, the module base is compatible with standard cold plates. I recommend proper gate drive design with desaturation protection for automotive safety applications.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance IGBT module ideal for EV inverter and high-power solar applications

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current for larger power inverters

faqs.question: What cooling is required for this module?
faqs.answer: The SWH150-1200H requires effective thermal management. For continuous operation at rated current: 1) Forced air cooling requires careful thermal analysis; 2) Liquid cooling (cold plate) is recommended for full-power operation; 3) Thermal resistance from junction to case is specified in datasheet; 4) Use thermal interface material with appropriate thickness; 5) Mounting torque must be specified (typically 3-5 Nm for M5 bolts). Always verify junction temperature stays below 150°C under worst-case conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use liquid cooling for full-power applications. Verify junction temperature under worst-case conditions.

categories.name: MOSFETs
categories.description: Fusemi MOSFETs provide efficient power switching for applications from 20V to 500V. These silicon MOSFETs offer low on-resistance, fast switching, and robust performance for power supplies, motor controls, and automotive applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Power MOSFETs
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting power MOSFETs based on voltage, current, RDS(on), and switching requirements.

faqs.question: What does RDS(on) mean?
faqs.answer: RDS(on) is the drain-source on-resistance when the MOSFET is fully enhanced. Lower RDS(on) means lower conduction losses and less heat generation. RDS(on) is specified at specific VGS (typically 10V). Always check RDS(on) at your actual gate drive voltage. RDS(on) increases with temperature - typically doubles from 25°C to 125°C.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select MOSFETs with RDS(on) low enough to keep conduction losses acceptable at operating temperature.

products.name: 40A 300V N-Channel MOSFET
products.shortDescription: 40A 300V N-channel MOSFET for power supplies and motor controls with low RDS(on).
descriptionParagraphs: The SMN40N30 is a high-performance N-channel MOSFET designed for power supply and motor control applications.
With 300V rating and 40A current capability, this MOSFET handles demanding power conversion applications.
The low RDS(on) ensures high efficiency and reduced heat generation.

faeReview.author: Dr. Steven Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Supply
faeReview.content: The SMN40N30 is a workhorse for 300V power supply applications. I've used this MOSFET extensively in PFC stages and main switching stages where the 65mΩ RDS(on) provides good efficiency without excessive cost. The 40A rating is sufficient for most single-phase power factor correction applications. The TO-247 package is industry standard and simplifies thermal management. For continuous conduction mode PFC, the switching losses are well-controlled. The avalanche energy rating provides robustness during startup and transients.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent all-around 300V MOSFET for power supply and motor control applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Higher power or higher efficiency applications

faqs.question: What gate resistor value is recommended?
faqs.answer: For the SMN40N30, typical gate resistor values: 5-20 ohms for standard operation. Lower values (5-10 ohms) for faster switching and higher efficiency. Higher values (15-20 ohms) for reduced EMI and ringing. Start with 10 ohms and optimize based on switching waveforms and thermal performance. For synchronous rectification, lower values may be needed for fast turn-off.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 10 ohms gate resistor. Adjust based on switching performance requirements.

categories.name: SiC Devices
categories.description: Fusemi SiC (Silicon Carbide) devices provide high-efficiency power conversion for EV, solar, and industrial applications. SiC MOSFETs and SiC diodes offer lower switching losses, higher operating temperatures, and higher breakdown voltages compared to traditional silicon devices.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select SiC Devices
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting SiC MOSFETs and diodes for high-efficiency power conversion applications.

faqs.question: Why choose SiC over silicon?
faqs.answer: SiC advantages: 1) Lower switching losses - critical for high-frequency applications; 2) Lower conduction losses - RDS(on) stays low at high temperatures; 3) Higher voltage capability - up to 1700V; 4) Higher temperature operation - junction to 200°C+; 5) Higher thermal conductivity - better heat spreading. SiC enables smaller, lighter, more efficient systems despite higher component cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SiC for high-efficiency, high-frequency, or high-temperature applications.

products.name: 30A 1200V SiC MOSFET
products.shortDescription: 30A 1200V SiC MOSFET for high-efficiency EV and solar applications with ultra-low switching losses.
descriptionParagraphs: The SC30N1200 is a high-performance SiC MOSFET designed for high-efficiency power conversion applications.
With 1200V rating and 30A current capability, this SiC device enables compact and efficient designs for EV and solar.
The ultra-low switching losses and fast body diode enable high-frequency operation with minimal losses.
features: 1200V rating for 800V EV and solar systems
Ultra-low switching losses for high efficiency
Fast body diode for synchronous rectification
Low gate charge for easy driving

faeReview.author: Dr. James Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: SiC is the future of high-efficiency power conversion, and the SC30N1200 represents excellent value for 800V applications. I've designed several EV chargers using this device where the switching losses are dramatically lower than silicon IGBTs. At 20kHz, the efficiency improvement is 2-3% compared to IGBTs, which translates to significant thermal management advantages. The body diode performance is surprisingly good - suitable for hard-switched topologies without auxiliary diodes in many cases. The gate drive requirements are similar to silicon MOSFETs, making migration from silicon relatively straightforward. I recommend 0-18V gate drive with 5-10 ohm gate resistors for optimal performance.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance 1200V SiC MOSFET ideal for 800V EV and solar applications

companionParts.description: Optimized gate driver for SiC MOSFETs

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is required?
faqs.answer: The SC30N1200 requires 0V/18V gate drive (enhanced at 18V). The threshold voltage is around 3.5-5V. Never drive above 20V or below -4V. Use dedicated SiC MOSFET gate drivers that can source/sink the required peak currents (typically 5-10A). The gate charge is lower than equivalent silicon MOSFETs, but switching speed requires capable drivers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 0V/18V gate drive with dedicated SiC drivers. Never exceed 20V.

categories.name: Power Modules
categories.description: Fusemi power modules integrate multiple semiconductors and functions into complete solutions including Integrated Power Modules (IPM) for motor control and Power Integrated Modules (PIM) for power conversion. These modules reduce design complexity and improve reliability.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Power Modules
selectionGuide.description: Guide for selecting integrated power modules for motor control and power conversion applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between IPM and PIM?
faqs.answer: IPM (Integrated Power Module) integrates IGBTs, MOSFETs, gate drivers, and protection circuits in one package for motor control. PIM (Power Integrated Module) integrates the rectifier, inverter, and brake chopper for complete power conversion. IPM is for the motor side, PIM is for the AC-DC-AC conversion.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use IPM for motor control. Use PIM for complete power conversion including rectification.

products.name: 25A 600V Integrated Power Module
products.shortDescription: 25A 600V IPM with integrated IGBTs, gate drivers, and protection for motor control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The IPM25-600S is a complete integrated power module designed for motor control applications.
With 600V rating and 25A current, this IPM integrates six IGBTs, gate drivers, bootstrap circuits, and protection.
The all-in-one solution reduces design complexity and improves system reliability.
features: Complete three-phase inverter in one package
Integrated gate drivers with desaturation protection
Easy to use - minimal external components

faeReview.author: Dr. Robert Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control
faeReview.content: IPMs are the easiest way to implement motor control without designing discrete power stages. The IPM25-600S is perfect for 1-3kW motor drives in appliances and industrial equipment. The integrated drivers simplify the design dramatically - just add microcontroller PWM signals. The protection features are comprehensive and well-designed. I've used this in several HVAC applications where the compact size and built-in protections saved significant board space. The thermal performance is adequate for many applications with proper heatsinking.
faeReview.highlight: Easy-to-use integrated power module for motor control applications

faqs.question: What external components are needed?
faqs.answer: The IPM25-600S requires minimal external components: 1) Power supply capacitors - bypass and bulk capacitors on DC link; 2) Bootstrap capacitors - one per high-side gate; 3) PWM signals from microcontroller - 3.3V or 5V logic compatible; 4) Current sense resistors - for over-current protection; 5) Heatsink - thermal management is critical. The datasheet provides complete application circuit examples. Keep gate drive traces short and separate from power traces.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow datasheet application circuit. Keep layouts compact and separated appropriately.
